results.title: Jin-Hoon Yang
highlights:
Data Scientist at Qnity Electronics
...
Data Scientist at [Qnity](https://www.linkedin.com/company/qnityelectronics)
...
With over 10 years of experience in Density Functional Theory (DFT) calculations, I have developed deep expertise in computational materials science, including analyzing electronic structures, stability, and surface reactions of various materials. Transitioning from my extensive DFT background, I am now focused on artificial intelligence research, developing accurate models to predict material properties and creating data platforms for managing and visualizing experimental data. I am particularly passionate about data-driven research and its potential to revolutionize materials discovery and development.
...
### Data Scientist at [Qnity](https://www.linkedin.com/company/qnityelectronics) (Current)
...
Semiconductor Manufacturing company
...
28
...
, South Korea,
...
My work includes developing data processing pipelines and machine learning models for predicting material properties. I have built artificial intelligence models for predicting material properties like band gap and glass transition temperature, utilizing transfer learning methods and Bayesian optimization for optimal results. I actively collaborate with experimental teams, using prediction models to rapidly screen experimental conditions and contribute to advancements in materials science research.
...
I am an experienced researcher with a strong background in materials discovery and artificial intelligence modeling. My work includes managing massive text data for materials discovery by developing semi-automated pipelines, creating natural language processing models for secondary batteries, and improving model performance with data management techniques. Additionally, I have explored neural network potentials for complex organic crystalline systems and conducted comprehensive density functional theory investigations for advanced materials, focusing on garnets as cathode active materials for high-energy-density batteries, black phosphorus surface recovery mechanisms, and fundamental property analysis for graphene nanostructures.
...

results.title: Cybersecurity Director | Jobflarely
highlights:
, implement, and lead a fit-for-purpose Cyber Operations Program that safeguards enterprise assets across IT and OT environments. This is a high-impact leadership role focused on building and optimizing our Cybersecurity Operations Program, including organizational design, staffing, and operational excellence. Recognized as a thought leader with deep expertise in Cybersecurity Operations, Incident Response, and Identity & Access Management. Key Responsibilities Applies comprehensive knowledge of organizational functions and business operations to differentiate Qnity from competitors. Develop and execute a cyber operations strategy and build organizational structure for SOC, vulnerability management, OT security, and threat intelligence. Oversee 24x7 SOC operations, including monitoring, detection, and incident response; Drive continuous improvement and maturity through playbooks and automation. Lead the enterprise vulnerability management programs across IT and OT environments and ensure timely identification, prioritization, and remediation of vulnerabilities. Lead and operationalize a Threat Intelligence strategy to anticipate and mitigate emerging threats. Set KPIs and SLAs to measure, align, and improve cyber operations performance Design for Scale: Build fit-for-purpose staffing models, governance, and KPIs to measure success. Ensure cybersecurity operations scale with business growth and digital transformation. Manage Identity Lifecycle & Governance: operate and mature identity and access management lifecycle, enable secure access (PAM, JIT, SSO, MFA), and keep on-prem/cloud integration running smoothly. Negotiates and influences senior levels to drive cybersecurity objectives and secure IT, OT and critical infrastructure. Proactively identifies potential risks, addresses operational challenges, and leads problem-solving initiatives to strengthen organizational resilience and enhance cybersecurity posture. Qualifications and Experience 10+ years in cybersecurity operations, with 5+ years in leadership roles. Proven track record in managing incident response and identity security operations across large-scale enterprise ecosystems. Expertise in vulnerability management, OT security, and threat intelligence. Strong knowledge of cybersecurity frameworks such as NIST CSF, MITRE ATT&CK, or similar. Relevant certifications (CISSP, CISM, GIAC, or equivalent) preferred. Strategic thinker with strong problem-solving skills and practical operational experience. Skilled at building high-performing teams and driving continuous improvement. Strong business acumen with ability to turn technical risk into clear business impact. Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills. #LI-LH1 #LI-Hybrid Join our Talent Community to stay connected with us! Qnity is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability or any other protected class. results.title: Site Electrical Engineer | Jobflarely
highlights:
Site Electrical Engineer | Jobflarely
...
# Site Electrical Engineer
...
Posted Jun 23, 2026
...
Are you looking to power the next leap in the exciting world of advanced electronics? Do you want to help solve problems that drive success in the rapidly evolving technology and connectivity landscape? Then bring your problem-solving, passion, and creativity to help us power the next leap in electronics. At Qnity, we’re more than a global leader in materials and solutions for advanced electronics and high-tech industries – we’re a tight-knit team that is motivated by new possibilities, and always up for a challenge. All our dedicated teams contribute to making cutting-edge technology possible. We value forward-thinking challengers, boundary-pushers, and diverse perspectives across all our departments, because we know we play a critical role in the world enabling faster progress for all. Learn how you can start or jumpstart your career with us. The Site Electrical Engineer is responsible for supporting the site Electrical Power Distribution (EPD) group, manages the power coordination of the site’s electrical power distribution system, serves as the site’s Electrical Safety Professional (ESP), and is a resource to all site personnel for electrical engineering related issues and questions. The key tasks for the Site Electrical Engineer include: Responsible for the management of the coordination of the relays, breakers, and fuses providing overcurrent and short circuit protection for the site electrical distribution system. Maintain the SKM computer system model of the site electrical distribution system. Calculate and provide labelling for the short circuit and arc flash values for the electrical distribution system. Provided technical support and guidance to the Electrical Power Distribution (EPD) maintenance group. Lead the site’s electrical safety program as the site Electrical Safety Professional (ESP) Organize and execute the site’s electrical safety audit program. Serve as the primary approver for the site’s electrical design adequacy reviews of new electrical designs Serve on the Site and Corporate Electrical Safety Teams Manage the site’s 5-year Electrical System Infrastructure plan Lead and complete the 5-year Site Electrical Hazards Analysis. Calculate and provide month-end accounting analysis of the site’s electricity usage. Provide technical support for all site electrical incidents. Serve as the primary technical resource for site electrical power system infrastructure upgrades and improvements. Requirements: BS Electrical Engineering, 3 years experience #LI-RS1 Join our Talent Community to stay connected with us! Qnity is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability or any other protected class. invoices meet compliance with internal policies, contractual terms, and regulatory requirements. Perform root-cause analysis for recurring errors and propose corrective actions. results.title: Site Production Planner | Hirequorum
highlights: Are you looking to power the next leap in the exciting world of advanced electronics? Do you want to help solve problems that drive success in the rapidly evolving technology and connectivity landscape? Then bring your problem-solving, passion, and creativity to help us power the next leap in electronics. At Qnity, we’re more than a global leader in materials and solutions for advanced electronics and high-tech industries – we’re a tight-knit team that is motivated by new possibilities, and always up for a challenge. All our dedicated teams contribute to making cutting-edge technology possible. We value forward-thinking challengers, boundary-pushers, and diverse perspectives across all our departments, because we know we play a critical role in the world enabling faster progress for all. Learn how you can start or jumpstart your career with us. The Production Planner is responsible for coordinating, scheduling, and monitoring the flow of materials and production activities to ensure that customer orders are completed on time, within budget, and according to quality standards. This role works closely with operations, procurement, engineering, and logistics to optimize production efficiency and minimize downtime. Key Responsibilities Develop and maintain detailed production schedules based on customer orders, forecasts, and capacity constraints. Adjust schedules in response to delays, equipment issues, or material shortages. Coordinate with department leads to ensure labor and equipment are aligned with schedule requirements. Review material requirements and ensure raw materials and components are available to meet production needs. Work with procurement to expedite or delay orders as needed. Maintain accurate inventory data and participate in cycle counts when required. Collaborate with operations, quality, engineering, and logistics to ensure smooth workflow. Monitor production performance against schedule; identify bottlenecks and propose improvements. Track and report key metrics such as on-time delivery (OTD), capacity utilization, and schedule adherence. Support process-improvement initiatives to increase efficiency and reduce lead times. Assist in developing best practices for planning, scheduling, and workflow optimization. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Operations Management, Business, or related field (preferred). 2–5 years of experience in production planning, scheduling, or supply chain within a manufacturing environment. Proficiency with ERP/MRP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, Epicor). Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities. Ability to manage multiple priorities and work in a fast-paced environment. Knowledge of lean manufacturing principles is a plus. #LI-RS1 Join our Talent Community to stay connected with us! Qnity is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability or any other protected class. If you need a reasonable accommodation to search or apply for a position, please visit our Accessibility Page for Contact Information. Qnity offers a comprehensive pay and benefits package. Qnity is a global leader in materials and solutions for advanced electronics and high-tech industries, seeking a Customer Service Representative. This role involves managing customer orders, providing solutions, and collaborating with various functional partners to enhance customer experience and meet operational needs. Responsibilities Manage customer orders through Customer Service work processes and systems Handle all types of customer orders, including indent orders and services invoicing requests Supports customers with coordination of returns when required, following Qnity’s Return Policies Maintain a thorough knowledge of the businesses’ products, businesses, applications, and service offerings Strengthen customer experience by developing solid relationships, understanding customer needs, and gathering market intelligence Coordinate activity at assigned customer accounts, proactively address issues, and implement solutions Weekly participation in meetings with the Supply Chain team to analyze possible material delays Identify and deliver value added services Proactively provide differentiated services to our customers by thorough understanding of our service offerings Differentiate between customer requirements and Qnity capabilities; choose the best/cost effective solution Support commercial strategies with Qnity customers across multiple businesses Provide backup coverage as necessary Responsible for establishing and maintaining relationships with the customers Respond to sales team inquiries as: prices, stock, latest sales, etc Skills Bachelor's degree or equivalent is preferred Develop proficiency as a Customer Service Representative Proven ability to build and maintain strong customer relationships Ability to adjust with ease to new conditions and personnel and to exhibit the competence and maturity to encourage confidence from Customers Ability to identify areas for improvement and escalate for change management implementation Excellent interpersonal and written communication skills Strong office skills Knowledge of the Order to Cash (OTC) transactions in SAP Intermediate to high level Microsoft Excel proficiency is required (including Excel, PowerPoint, Word, and Power BI) Demonstrated skills: multitasking, project management including the ability to translate needs into a workable implementation plan, change management Demonstrated flexibility/adaptability in a dynamic, fast-paced team environment Salesforce.com experience is a plus Logistics, operations, or manufacturing experience preferred Benefits Comprehensive pay and benefits package Company Overview Qnity is a premier technology provider across the semiconductor value chain, empowering AI, high performance computing, and advanced connectivity. results.title: Regional Supply Chain Manager | Remotepulse
highlights:
leader in materials and solutions for advanced electronics and high-tech industries – we’re a tight-knit team that is motivated by new possibilities, and always up for a challenge. All our dedicated teams contribute to making cutting-edge technology possible. We value forward-thinking challengers, boundary-pushers, and diverse perspectives across all our departments, because we know we play a critical role in the world enabling faster progress for all. Learn how you can start or jumpstart your career with us. Role Overview: We are seeking an experienced Regional Supply Chain Manager to lead and optimize end‑to‑end supply chain operations for our Advanced Film Technologies (AFT) business. In this role, you will drive strategic procurement, efficient inventory management, and reliable supply to ensure our products meet customer expectations. Success requires strong analytical capability, exceptional planning skills, and the agility to thrive in a dynamic market environment. Key Responsibilities: Develop and execute supply chain strategies to support AFT business objectives. Manage procurement activities, ensuring reliable supply, and strong supplier/contract manufacturing relationships. Monitor and control inventory levels using forecasted supply/demand to prevent shortages and minimize excess. Oversee logistics and distribution processes to meet production schedules and customer delivery timelines. Analyze supply chain KPIs and lead initiatives that enhance efficiency, cost performance, and service levels. Partner closely with production, finance, quality, engineering, and other cross‑functional teams to maintain alignment and operational excellence. Communicate clearly and effectively with internal and external stakeholders, ensuring timely, accurate, and transparent information sharing across all supply chain activities. Qualifications & Skills: Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, Business Administration, or related field. 5+ years of supply chain management experience, preferably in films/laminates or manufacturing environments. Strong knowledge of planning/scheduling, inventory control, and manufacturing operations. Proficiency in SAP, OMP, and supply chain analytics tools. Excellent problem‑solving, analytical, and decision‑making skills. Ability to operate effectively in a fast‑paced environment with shifting priorities. Professional supply chain certification (APICS - CSCP/CPIM). Exceptional communication skills with a strong focus on accuracy, clarity, and data integrity. #LI-LA1 加入我们的人才社区 并与我们保持联系！https://careers.qnityelectronics.com/cn/zh/jointalentcommunity Qnity是一家推崇机会均等的公司。我们对符合条件的求职者一视同仁，不会因其种族、肤色、宗教、信条、性别、性取向、性别认同、婚姻状况、国籍、年龄、退伍军人身份、残疾或或属于任何其他受保护阶层而区别对待。如果您需要借助合理的便利条件来搜索或申请职位，请访问我们的无障碍功能页面获取联系信息。http://www.qnityelectronics/accessibility.html Qnity提供全面的薪资和福利方案。要了解更多，请访问薪酬和福利页面 https://careers.qnityelectronics.com/cn/zh/compensation-and-benefits-final 我們使用人工智慧（AI）來增強我們的招聘流程。 Join a global leader making tomorrow’s technologies possible, from AI to advanced computing and beyond. results.title: Site Production Planner | Joblume
highlights: Are you looking to power the next leap in the exciting world of advanced electronics? Do you want to help solve problems that drive success in the rapidly evolving technology and connectivity landscape? Then bring your problem-solving, passion, and creativity to help us power the next leap in electronics. At Qnity, we’re more than a global leader in materials and solutions for advanced electronics and high-tech industries – we’re a tight-knit team that is motivated by new possibilities, and always up for a challenge. All our dedicated teams contribute to making cutting-edge technology possible. We value forward-thinking challengers, boundary-pushers, and diverse perspectives across all our departments, because we know we play a critical role in the world enabling faster progress for all. Learn how you can start or jumpstart your career with us. The Production Planner is responsible for coordinating, scheduling, and monitoring the flow of materials and production activities to ensure that customer orders are completed on time, within budget, and according to quality standards. This role works closely with operations, procurement, engineering, and logistics to optimize production efficiency and minimize downtime. Key Responsibilities Develop and maintain detailed production schedules based on customer orders, forecasts, and capacity constraints. Adjust schedules in response to delays, equipment issues, or material shortages. Coordinate with department leads to ensure labor and equipment are aligned with schedule requirements. Review material requirements and ensure raw materials and components are available to meet production needs. Work with procurement to expedite or delay orders as needed. Maintain accurate inventory data and participate in cycle counts when required. Collaborate with operations, quality, engineering, and logistics to ensure smooth workflow. Monitor production performance against schedule; identify bottlenecks and propose improvements. Track and report key metrics such as on-time delivery (OTD), capacity utilization, and schedule adherence. Support process-improvement initiatives to increase efficiency and reduce lead times. Assist in developing best practices for planning, scheduling, and workflow optimization. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Operations Management, Business, or related field (preferred). 2–5 years of experience in production planning, scheduling, or supply chain within a manufacturing environment. Proficiency with ERP/MRP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, Epicor). Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities. Ability to manage multiple priorities and work in a fast-paced environment. Knowledge of lean manufacturing principles is a plus. #LI-RS1 Join our Talent Community to stay connected with us! Qnity is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability or any other protected class. results.title: What is Qnity Electronics, Inc. (Q) stock_business overview_development history
highlights:
The company specializes in advanced materials and solutions, including CMP pads, photoresists, and interconnect materials essential for AI, high-performance computing, and advanced connectivity. In 2025, Qnity reported strong net sales of
...
, a 10% year-over-year increase, with GAAP net income of
...
million. Its
...
as of early 2026.
...
In early 2026, Qnity announced its "Green Silicon" initiative, aiming to reduce the power consumption of its data centre chips by 30% over the next two years. Additionally, the company has expanded its footprint into the "Edge AI" market, launching the Q-Nexus 9 chip, designed specifically for on-device machine learning in autonomous drones and robotics.
...
### 3. High-Growth Phase: The AI and Automation Era (2023 - Present)
...
The post-2023 period saw Qnity capitalize on the global demand for localized intelligence. By acquiring "SensorTech Alpha" in late 2024, Qnity integrated advanced lidar processing capabilities into its portfolio. In 2025, the company reported a record-breaking fiscal year, with revenue surpassing the $1.5 billion mark for the first time.
...
Qnity is aggressively expanding its production capacity to meet the surging global demand for chips. Significant investments include:• Delaware Facility: Recently opened a 385,000-square-foot advanced manufacturing facility in Newark, DE, to support AI and high-performance computing (HPC) materials.• Taiwan Investment: Committed
...
61.5 million to a new research and manufacturing facility in Taiwan, strengthening
...
"local-for-local" service model in
...
heart of the global semiconductor hub
...
The company’s growth is anchored in Advanced Nodes (3nm and below) and Advanced Packaging. Qnity has collaborated with industry giants like NVIDIA to accelerate innovation in materials for next-generation AI chips. As the industry moves toward a $1 trillion market by 2030, Qnity’s position as a provider of critical consumables (90% of its portfolio is unit-driven) ensures it scales with wafer starts and volume growth rather than just capital equipment cycles.
...
• AI-Driven Demand: Qnity is a primary beneficiary of the AI infrastructure boom, providing essential materials for lithography, thermal management, and advanced interconnects used in AI accelerators.• Shareholder Returns: The Board has authorized a $500 million share repurchase program and established a quarterly dividend (recently $0.08 per share), signaling confidence in long-term cash flow.• Pure-Play Focus: As a standalone entity separate from DuPont, Qnity can now dedicate 100% of its resources to the fast-moving electronics and semiconductor sectors, improving operational agility.
...
Qnity Electronics, Inc. is a leading pure-play technology solutions provider for the semiconductor and electronics industries. It was established as an independent public company in November 2025 following its spin-off from DuPont de Nemours.Investment Highlights:1. AI and High-Performance Computing (HPC) Exposure: The company is a critical supplier of materials like chemical mechanical planarization (CMP) pads and slurries, which are essential for manufacturing advanced AI chips.2. Market Leadership: Qnity holds a strong position in the semiconductor value chain, particularly in advanced packaging and interconnect solutions.3. Strategic Collaborations: In March 2026, the company announced a collaboration with NVIDIA to accelerate innovation in semiconductor materials.Main Competitors:Qnity competes with other specialty chemical and semiconductor material suppliers such as Entegris (ENTG), CMC Materials (now part of Entegris), and Versum Materials (now part of Merck KGaA). In the interconnect segment, it faces competition from firms like MKS Instruments and Amkor Technology.
...

NVIDIA) provides a tailwind for

to bolster domestic supply chains

results.title: FinancialContent - Qnity Powers the Transition from Shrink to Stack with Advanced Packaging Solutions
highlights:
Qnity Electronics, Inc. (“Qnity”) (NYSE: Q), a premier technology solutions leader across the semiconductor value chain, today announced the launch of its Advanced Packaging Innovation Hub, underscoring advanced packaging as a strategic growth driver and innovation priority for the company. The new online platform showcases Qnity’s breadth of material and technology solutions designed for advanced packaging, enabling the next generation of artificial intelligence (AI), high-performance computing, cloud, networking, and edge computing systems.
...
The semiconductor industry is moving beyond the traditional gains of Moore's Law, and innovation is increasingly shifting from "shrink" to "stack" through the design of advanced 3D architectures. Advanced packaging is one of the most important enabling technologies for AI infrastructure and next-generation computing, making it a key focus area for Qnity's long-term growth and innovation strategy.
...
“As AI reshapes computing, the hardest engineering problems are moving into the connections between chips, layer to layer — where performance, power, density, and reliability are decided," said Chuck Xu, President, Interconnect Solutions at Qnity. "That's where Qnity shines. We bring our semiconductor and interconnect strengths together so customers can master advanced packaging from design through system integration, end-to-end.”
...
architectures evolve beyond conventional
...
has emerged as a critical driver
...
manufacturers face growing demands for high
...
Explore Qnity's Advanced Packaging Innovation Hub to learn how the company's solutions are enabling the future of AI-driven computing.
...
About Qnity Qnity is a premier technology solutions provider across the semiconductor value chain, empowering AI, high performance computing, and advanced connectivity. From groundbreaking solutions for semiconductor chip manufacturing, to enabling high-speed transmission within complex electronic systems, our high-performance materials and integration expertise make tomorrow’s technologies possible. More information about the company, its businesses and solutions can be found at www.qnityelectronics.com.

results.title: Qnity Powers the Transition from Shrink to Stack with Advanced Packaging Solutions
highlights:
WILMINGTON, Del. -- June 22, 2026 -- Qnity Electronics, Inc. (“Qnity”) (NYSE: Q), a premier technology solutions leader across the semiconductor value chain, today announced the launch of its Advanced Packaging Innovation Hub, underscoring advanced packaging as a strategic growth driver and innovation priority for the company. The new online platform showcases Qnity’s breadth of material and technology solutions designed for advanced packaging, enabling the next generation of artificial intelligence (AI), high-performance computing, cloud, networking, and edge computing systems.
...
The semiconductor industry is moving beyond the traditional gains of Moore's Law, and innovation is increasingly shifting from "shrink" to "stack" through the design of advanced 3D architectures. Advanced packaging is one of the most important enabling technologies for AI infrastructure and next-generation computing, making it a key focus area for Qnity's long-term growth and innovation strategy.
...
“As AI reshapes computing, the hardest engineering problems are moving into the connections between chips, layer to layer — where performance, power, density, and reliability are decided," said Chuck Xu, President, Interconnect Solutions at Qnity. "That's where Qnity shines. We bring our semiconductor and interconnect strengths together so customers can master advanced packaging from design through system integration, end-to-end.”
...
